ogTitle: Cox Peninsula vs Daguragu: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cox Peninsula and Daguragu.
heading: Cox Peninsula vs Daguragu — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cox Peninsula, Northern Territory and Daguragu, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cox Peninsula has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Daguragu (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cox Peninsula or Daguragu safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cox Peninsula has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Daguragu scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cox Peninsula scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Daguragu scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cox Peninsula or Daguragu?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cox Peninsula has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
